title: Understand: the moon and its motions
description: Check your understanding of the moon and its motions in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Middle school Earth and space science - NGSS
Understand: the moon and its motions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is the best description of the moon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a body that revolves around Earth and generates light
a body that revolves around the sun and generates light
a body that revolves around Earth and reflects light
a body that revolves around the sun and reflects light

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is the best description of the moon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an object that orbits Earth and creates light
an object that orbits Earth and reflects light
an object that orbits the sun and creates light
an object that orbits the sun and reflects light

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which two characteristics best describe the moon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: generates light
reflects light
revolves around Earth
revolves around the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why do we see a full moon about once a month?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The moon orbits Earth about once every $27$ days.
The moon orbits the sun about once every $27$ days.
The moon turns on its axis about once every $27$ days.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why do the phases of the moon repeat every month?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Every $27$ days, the moon completes one revolution around Earth.
Every $27$ days, the moon completes one revolution around the sun.
Every $27$ days, the moon completes one rotation on its axis.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why do we see each moon phase only about once a month?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It takes about $27$ days for the moon to rotate on its axis once.
It takes about $27$ days for the moon to orbit Earth once.
It takes about $27$ days for the moon to orbit the sun once.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens during a lunar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The shadow of Earth falls on the moon.
The shadow of the moon falls on Earth.
The shadow of the sun falls on the moon.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens during a lunar eclipse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Earth blocks direct sunlight from reaching the moon.
The moon blocks direct sunlight from reaching Earth.
The moon gets hotter from direct sunlight, causing it to turn red.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens during a lunar eclipse?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Earth moves into the shadow of the moon.
The moon moves into the shadow of Earth.
The moon moves into the shadow of the sun.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When does the moon appear completely lit from Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: during a new moon
during a first quarter moon
during a full moon

assessment_items.question_content:
**When does the moon appear completely dark from Earth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: during a new moon
during a full moon
during a third quarter moon

assessment_items.question_content:
**The moon appears completely lit from Earth during which moon phase?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: new moon
full moon
third quarter moon
